calendar.matters:
ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Dec-20-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Order of Examination as to Debtor Kai Tang.

Dept. 514; Judge: Michelle Tong; Clerk: N. Webb; Bailiff: Deputy Tauscher; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 3:07:45 p.m. and concluding at 3:32:00 p.m.

Shanshan Zou Esq appearing for Creditor Canjiang Wu.
Debtor Kai Tang is present, in pro per, placed under oath and examined.

After one hour of examination, the parties reach an impasse and seek intervention from the Court. The Court hears testimony, has reviewed all the evidence, and after hearing the testimony of the parties, and reviewing the evidence presented, The Court orders Debtor Kai Tang to make monthly payments as follows; Commencing on February 25, 2023 and to be made on the 25th day of each month thereafter Debtor Kai Tang shall pay $4000.00 each month until the balance of $290,684.64 is paid in full. (514)

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Oct-11-2022 Continued To Dec-20-22 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 514
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for order of examination.

Dept. 514; Judge: Murlene J. Randle; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Not Recorded

Plaintiff Canjiang Wu represented by Shanshan Zou, Esq.
Defendant Kai Cheng Tang appearing in pro per.

KAI CHENG TANG is placed under oath and examined in open court.

Plaintiff requests a continuance for additional documentation and examination of Defendant.
Court orders matter continued to Dec-20-2022 at 2:00 p.m. in Department 514 for further order of examination.

Notice Of Application And Hearing With Court Reporter Fee
Off Calendar Jun-08-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CANJIANG WU's Notice Of Application And Hearing fro Right to Attach Order/Order for Issuance of Writ of Attachment:

Plaintiff Wu's application for a right to attach order and writ attachment is granted in the amount of $205,120. Defendant Kai Cheng Tang's claim of exemption is conditionally denied.

Code of Civil Procedure section 484.090 provides that the court "shall issue" a right to attach order to the plaintiff where the following elements are established: (1) the claim upon which the attachment is based is one upon which an attachment may be issued; (2) the applicant has established "the probable validity" of the claim upon which the attachment is based; (3) the attachment is not sought for a purpose other than the recovery on the claim upon which the request for attachment is based; and (4) the amount to be secured by the attachment is greater than zero. In addition, to obtain a writ of attachment, the plaintiff must establish that the "total amount of the claim or claims is a fixed or readily ascertainable amount." (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 483.010(a); CIT Group/Equip. Fin., Inc. v. Super DVD, Inc. (2004) 115 Cal.App.4th 537, 540.)

Here, plaintiff meets the requirements for attachment. Plaintiff's cause of action for breach of the note (cause of action 3) is a commercial claim that is subject to attachment per CCP 483.010(c). Plaintiff establishes the probable validity of that claim because the loan repayment obligation accrued in May of 2019 and defendant has yet to repay the loan. (Wu Decl., pars. 9-12; Ex. B.) The duty to repay the loan under the note is not subject to a condition precedent. Plaintiff established irreparable harm under Code of Civil Procedure section 485.010(b)(2) as defendant has not been repaying his debts and is apparently insolvent. The amount due is ascertainable and plaintiff has met all of the other requirements for attachment.

Plaintiff shall provide a Judicial Council writ of attachment order for execution. (See Judicial Council Order AT-120.)

Defendant has filed a claim of exemption, but that claim does not conform to the requirements of the Code, nor does it include the required financial statement. (See Code Civ. Proc. secs. 703.520, 703.530.) The right to attach order is issued as to all property subject to attachment. (See Code Civ. Proc. sec. 487.010(c).) If Plaintiff seeks to attach assets that are exempt from attachment, Defendant may file a notice of claim of exemption and the Court will hold a hearing as provided by the Code. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 703.550, 703.570, 703.580.)

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed Judicial Council order.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
